channel.title: KiddieDaily — News for Families
channel.description: Daily kid-friendly news with bias indicators and fact checks. Updated every morning.

item.title: Reigning champion Argentina escapes with remarkable World Cup victory over Egypt
item.description: Egypt was leading late, up 2-0. The Argentinians looked beaten. But they fought and fought and fought. Scoring one goal, then another to equalize. And, finally, a third to advance to the quarterfinal. — 6 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: NASA's Hubble spots a stellar sparkler for the Fourth of July
item.description: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured a spectacular red, white, and blue view of one of the Milky Way's oldest star clusters to celebrate the nation's 250th anniversary. Hidden within the ancient — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Scientists say Beefalo are all beef, no -alo. Breeders disagree
item.description: A whole-genome analysis of Beefalo, a hybrid bison-cattle breed, suggests very few individuals have any bison DNA at all, a new study reports. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Strange Spheres Washed Ashore on an Australian Beach. Authorities Say They're Probably 'Space Balls'—Empty Fuel Vessels From a Rocket
item.description: The shiny objects—each roughly twice the size of a basketball—began to appear on July 3. Authorities initially worried that they were hazardous but have since determined that they're likely safe bits — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover featuring his personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026, on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. For 2 — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Neanderthals and modern humans may have shared culture 59,000 years ago in Turkey, study finds
item.description: Fossils, stone tools and seashells in Turkey show that Neanderthals and the Homo sapiens who moved in later had the same hunting strategies and symbolic traditions even without overlapping at the site — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Alien life on nearby 'super Earth' much likelier than we thought, study claims
item.description: A recently discovered "super Earth" located around 25 light-years from our planet is not as massive as previously thought, raising the chances that it has the conditions to support life. — 3 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa
item.description: Using satellite data, researchers connected harmful algal blooms with warm water and low water levels at one of Colorado’s largest reservoirs. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Euclid telescope discovers the 2 most ancient monster black holes in the universe ‪—‬ each brighter than a trillion suns
item.description: A collection of newfound objects discovered by the Euclid telescope more than doubles the number of known quasars from the universe's first billion years. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: AI labels a lot of stuff as alien life
item.description: ‘It’s a very serious vulnerability.’ The post AI labels a lot of stuff as alien life appeared first on Popular Science . — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Comet 3I/ATLAS is older than the sun
item.description: At over 9 billion years old, the rare interstellar visitor is more than twice the sun's age. The post Comet 3I/ATLAS is older than the sun appeared first on Popular Science . — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: In pictures: The US celebrates its 250th birthday
item.description: The anniversary, known as Independence Day, featured huge parades, colourful fireworks and plenty of events in towns and cities throughout America. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: How did YOU watch the England game?
item.description: Whether you woke up early to catch-up, had a special watch party at your school or just caught the highlights - we want to know how YOU watched the epic match? Take part in our vote and let us know in — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are married!
item.description: A spokesperson for the couple confirmed the news after the ceremony, which took place on Friday. Find out more here. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Unidentified metal spheres found on Australian beach are 'debris from a foreign rocket body', space agency says
item.description: The Australian Space Agency is warning residents of Queensland to be on the lookout after metallic-looking spheres, possibly space debris, appeared on a beach. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: NASA celebrates America's 250th birthday with incredible views of space
item.description: NASA is marking the United States' 250th birthday with four striking red, white, and blue images of deep space from the Chandra X-ray Observatory. The collection features an exploded star, a stellar n — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Our Milky Way galaxy might be larger than we thought
item.description: Astronomers used cosmic explosions to find that we may be wrong about our own galaxy. — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Astronaut flexes his muscles mid-spacewalk | Space photo of the day for July 7, 2026
item.description: NASA astronaut Chris Williams recently flexed his muscles for a photo while on a spacewalk with fellow astronaut Jessica Meir. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Speaking More Languages May Help Slow Brain Aging
item.description: A new study suggests multilingual people have younger brains The post Speaking More Languages May Help Slow Brain Aging appeared first on Nautilus . — 2 outlets covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Rare Medieval Book of Hours to Headline Manuscript Sale
item.description: A beautifully illuminated fifteenth-century Book of Hours created in Bruges will be one of the highlights of Freeman's third annual Western Manuscripts and Miniatures sale, taking place on 9 July. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.
